---
title: "simpsons_trial"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)
library(bayesreg)
library(cmdstanr)
```

```{r}
blrme_model <- cmdstan_model("~/Documents/documents-main/research/rice_research/projects/meBART/codebase/meBART/vignettes/schluter_etal_immune/blrme_model.stan")
```

```{r}

## Use this code to load in specific data files
cWBC <- read_csv("./data/cWBC.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
cMISAMPLES <- read_csv("./data/cMISAMPLES.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
cHCTMETA <- read_csv("./data/cHCTMETA.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
```

# Eday vs Inverse Simpson Index

```{r}

# Remove patients with multiple BMT dates
df_bmt_dates <- cWBC %>%
    select(c(pid, `anon-bmt-date`)) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    add_count(`pid`) %>%
    filter(n == 1) %>%
    select(-n)

# Get list of unique PBSC patients
PBSC_patients <- cHCTMETA %>%
    filter(`hct-source` == "PBSC") %>%
    distinct(`pid`) %>%
    pull(`pid`)

# Get inverseSimpson
df_MISAMPLES <- cMISAMPLES %>%
    select(c(pid, anon_date, inverseSimpson)) %>%
    full_join(df_bmt_dates, by=c("pid")) %>%
    drop_na() %>%
    mutate(
        diff = abs(anon_date - `anon-bmt-date`),
    ) %>%
    group_by(pid) %>%
    slice_min(diff, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(diff < 7) %>%
    select(c(pid, inverseSimpson))


df_WBC <- cWBC %>%
    filter(eday > 0, is.na(exclude_reason)) %>%
    select(c(`pid`, `eday`)) %>%
    distinct()


df <- df_WBC %>% 
    full_join(df_MISAMPLES, by=c("pid")) %>%
    drop_na() %>%
    filter(pid %in% PBSC_patients)

# df$eday <- log(df$eday)
df$inverseSimpson <- log(df$inverseSimpson)

 
```

```{r}

ggplot(df, aes(x=inverseSimpson, y=eday)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method="lm") +
    labs(
        x = "inverse_Simpson",
        y = "E Day",
        title = "E Day vs Inverse Simpson"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()


mdl <- lm(eday ~ inverseSimpson, data=df)
summary(mdl)


```

```{r}
set.seed(0)

df <- df %>% 
    sample_frac(1) %>% # randomly shuffle rows
    mutate(fold = rep(1:5, length.out = n())) # partition into 5 folds


```

## Run all models


```{r}
mse_results <- data.frame(
    Fold = integer(),
    MSE_BLR = double(),
    MSE_BLRME = double(),
    MSE_RF = double(),
    MSE_BART = double()
)

for (i in 1:5){

    df_train <- df %>% filter(fold != i)
    df_test <- df %>% filter(fold == i)
    
    mdl_BART <- BART::wbart(df_train$inverseSimpson, df_train$eday, df_test$inverseSimpson)
    
    mdl_rf <- randomForest(eday ~ inverseSimpson, data = df_train, ntree = 200)

    mdl_blr <- bayesreg(eday ~ inverseSimpson, data=df_train, n.samples=1000, burnin=100, n.cores=1)
    
    blrme_data <- list(x_meas = df_train$inverseSimpson, y = df_train$eday, N=length(df_train$eday), tau=sqrt(0.2),
                   x_test=df_test$inverseSimpson, N_test=length(df_test$inverseSimpson))
# , 
#                    x_func=x_vals, N_func=100,
#                    x_nonoise=df_test$mean_inverseSimpson, N_nonoise=params$n/2) 

    blrme_model_fit <- blrme_model$sample(
        data = blrme_data,
        chains = 1,
        parallel_chains = 1,
        iter_warmup = 100,
        iter_sampling = 1000
    )
    
    preds_BART <- mdl_BART$yhat.test.mean
    mse_BART <- mean((df_test$eday - preds_BART)^2)
    
    preds_rf <- predict(mdl_rf, newdata = df_test)
    mse_rf <- mean((df_test$eday - preds_rf)^2)
    
    preds_blr <- predict(mdl_blr, df_test)[,1]
    mse_blr <- mean((df_test$eday - preds_blr)^2)
    
    blrme_preds <- colMeans(blrme_model_fit$draws("y_test", format="matrix"))
    mse_blrme <- mean((df_test$eday - blrme_preds)^2)
    
        
    
    
    
    mse_results <- rbind(mse_results, data.frame(
    Fold = i,
    MSE_BLR = mse_blr,
    MSE_BLRME = mse_blrme,
    MSE_RF = mse_rf,
    MSE_BART = mse_BART
))
    
    
}
    
    
    
    
    
```











```{r}

for (i in 1:5){

    df_train <- df %>% filter(fold != i)
    df_test <- df %>% filter(fold == i)

    mdl_meBART <- meBART::mebart_cont(df_train$inverseSimpson, df_train$eday,
                                    meas_error_sigma = matrix(0.2),
                                    x_mu = matrix(mean(df_train$inverseSimpson)),
                                    x_sigma = matrix(var(df_train$inverseSimpson)))


    preds_meBART <- predict(mdl_meBART, as.matrix(df_test$inverseSimpson))
    mse_meBART <- mean((df_test$eday - colMeans(preds_meBART))^2)
    
    mse_results$MSE_meBART[i] <- mse_meBART


}
    
    
```





# Both PP and mean plots

```{r}

ci_data <- data.frame(
    X = seq(min(df$inverseSimpson), max(df$inverseSimpson), length.out=100)
)

preds <- predict(mdl_BART, as.matrix(ci_data$X))

ci_data$y <- colMeans(preds)
ci_data$lower_CI <- apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs=0.025)
ci_data$upper_CI <- apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs=0.975)

preds2 <- preds + rnorm(nrow(preds)*ncol(preds), 0, 0.255)

ci_data2 <- data.frame(
    X = seq(min(df$inverseSimpson), max(df$inverseSimpson), length.out=100),
    lower_CI <- apply(preds2, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI <- apply(preds2, 2, quantile, probs=0.975)
)

# geom_line(aes(x=X, y=y), linetype=3) +
#         geom_ribbon(aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.2) +



ggplot() +
    geom_point(data=df, aes(x=inverseSimpson, y=eday)) +
    geom_line(data=ci_data, aes(x=X, y=y), color="blue") +
    geom_ribbon(data=ci_data, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.2, fill="blue") +
    geom_ribbon(data=ci_data2, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.1, fill="red") +
    geom_smooth(method="lm") +
    labs(
        x = "inverse_Simpson",
        y = "Eday",
        title = "Eday vs Inverse Simpson"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
```



```{r}

ci_data <- data.frame(
    X = seq(min(df$inverseSimpson), max(df$inverseSimpson), length.out=100)
)

preds <- predict(mdl_meBART, as.matrix(ci_data$X))

ci_data$y <- colMeans(preds)
ci_data$lower_CI <- apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs=0.025)
ci_data$upper_CI <- apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs=0.975)

preds2 <- preds + rnorm(nrow(preds)*ncol(preds), 0, 0.252)

ci_data2 <- data.frame(
    X = seq(min(df$inverseSimpson), max(df$inverseSimpson), length.out=100),
    lower_CI <- apply(preds2, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI <- apply(preds2, 2, quantile, probs=0.975)
)

# geom_line(aes(x=X, y=y), linetype=3) +
#         geom_ribbon(aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.2) +



ggplot() +
    geom_point(data=df, aes(x=inverseSimpson, y=eday)) +
    geom_line(data=ci_data, aes(x=X, y=y), color="blue") +
    geom_ribbon(data=ci_data, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.2, fill="blue") +
    geom_ribbon(data=ci_data2, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.1, fill="red") +
    geom_smooth(method="lm") +
    labs(
        x = "inverse_Simpson",
        y = "Eday",
        title = "Eday vs Inverse Simpson"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
```


# Combining both plots

```{r}

xs <- seq(min(df$inverseSimpson), max(df$inverseSimpson), length.out=1000)

preds_BART <- predict(mdl_BART, as.matrix(xs))

mean_ci_data_BART <- data.frame(
    X = xs,
    y = colMeans(preds_BART),
    lower_CI = apply(preds_BART, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI = apply(preds_BART, 2, quantile, probs=0.975),
    method = "BART"
)

preds_meBART <- predict(mdl_meBART, as.matrix(xs))

mean_ci_data_meBART <- data.frame(
    X = xs,
    y = colMeans(preds_meBART),
    lower_CI = apply(preds_meBART, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI = apply(preds_meBART, 2, quantile, probs=0.975),
    method = "meBART"
)

mean_ci_data <- rbind(mean_ci_data_BART, mean_ci_data_meBART)

BART_sigmas <- mdl_BART$sigma[101:1100]

preds_BART2 <- preds_BART + matrix(rnorm(nrow(preds_BART)*ncol(preds_BART), 0, BART_sigmas), nrow=nrow(preds_BART), ncol=ncol(preds_BART))

pp_ci_data_BART <- data.frame(
    X = xs,
    lower_CI = apply(preds_BART2, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI = apply(preds_BART2, 2, quantile, probs=0.975),
    method = "BART"
)

meBART_sigmas <- mdl_meBART$sigma[101:1100]

preds_meBART2 <- preds_meBART + matrix(rnorm(nrow(preds_meBART)*ncol(preds_meBART), 0, meBART_sigmas), nrow=nrow(preds_meBART), ncol=ncol(preds_meBART))

pp_ci_data_meBART <- data.frame(
    X = xs,
    lower_CI = apply(preds_meBART2, 2, quantile, probs=0.025),
    upper_CI = apply(preds_meBART2, 2, quantile, probs=0.975),
    method = "meBART"
)

pp_ci_data <- rbind(pp_ci_data_BART, pp_ci_data_meBART)

# geom_line(aes(x=X, y=y), linetype=3) +
#         geom_ribbon(aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI), alpha=0.2) +
```



```{r}
ggplot() +
    # 1. The Points (No changes needed here unless you want them in the legend)
    geom_point(data=df, aes(x=inverseSimpson, y=eday), alpha=0.25, size=1) +

    # 2. The Mean Line
    # Map 'color' to a specific string inside aes() to create a legend entry
    geom_line(data=mean_ci_data, aes(x=X, y=y, color="Mean Estimate")) +

    # 3. The Mean Ribbon
    # Map 'fill' to a string inside aes()
    geom_ribbon(data=mean_ci_data, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI, fill="Mean 95% CI"), alpha=0.2) +

    # 4. The Posterior Predictive Ribbon
    # Map 'fill' to a different string inside aes()
    geom_ribbon(data=pp_ci_data, aes(x=X, ymin=lower_CI, ymax=upper_CI, fill="Post. Pred. 95% CI"), alpha=0.1) +

    facet_wrap(~method) +
    
    # 5. Define the colors manually
    # We assign the color blue to "Mean Estimate"
    scale_color_manual(name = "", values = c("Mean Estimate" = "blue")) +
    
    # We assign blue and red to the specific fill labels we created above
    scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c("Mean 95% CI" = "blue", "Post. Pred. 95% CI" = "red")) +

    labs(
        x = "Inverse Simpson Index (log scale)",
        y = "Days Until Neutrophil Engraftment",
        title = "Engraftment Day vs Inverse Simpson Index"
    ) +
    theme_light() + 
    theme(
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = "black"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, color="black"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face="bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size=12),
        axis.title = element_text(size=12, face="bold"),
        # Optional: Place legend at the bottom or top if it takes up too much side space
        legend.position = "bottom" 
    ) +
    # 6. Override the alpha in the legend so the colors are visible
    # (Otherwise the legend squares might look too faint because of your alpha=0.1 setting)
    guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 0.5)))

ggsave("edayVsSimpson.png", width = 7, height = 4, dpi=600)
```
